note: Curated question->subtopic backfill from the Hart questionnaire. question blank where the competency is an interviewer judgement.

Subtopic01.1.question: What is the biggest achievable ambition for Hart in the next two years? Also, what are 1 or 2 major challenges to achieving this ambition?
Subtopic01.1.purpose: Clearly understands strategy: deep understanding and realistic (high) vs vague and tactical (low). Understand how they think about Hart's future, their strategic instincts, and their ability to plan and act with foresight.

Subtopic01.2.purpose: Ownership of strategy: deep ownership & proposing initiatives (high) vs poor ownership & alignment (low).

Subtopic01.3.purpose: Active driver or passive: energised long-term thinker (high) vs disengaged or deferential (low).

Subtopic01.4.question: Have you noticed any shifts in what clients or venues are asking for, or changes in the kinds of campaigns that are landing well?
Subtopic01.4.purpose: Industry sensitivity: aware of changing market needs and customer expectations (high) vs static view or only reacts after changes occur (low). Gauge sensitivity to shifts in client needs, campaign success, and broader industry trends.

Subtopic01.5.question: What do you think we'll need to adapt to in the next year or two to stay ahead?
Subtopic01.5.purpose: Forward-looking thinking: anticipates trends and future action (high) vs focused only on present or past activity (low).

Subtopic01.6.purpose: Commercial acumen: links market shifts to strategy and opportunity (high) vs disconnected from commercial context (low).

Subtopic02.1.question: What do you think sets Hart apart from your competitors?
Subtopic02.1.purpose: Market positioning clarity: clearly articulates Hart's differences (high) vs provides vague or generic responses (low). Understand their view of Hart's market position.

Subtopic02.2.question: Who do you see as your main competitors?
Subtopic02.2.purpose: Awareness of competitive dynamics: identifies competitors & real trends (high) vs out-of-date, narrow, or unaware (low).

Subtopic02.3.purpose: Strategic insight: clear about brand value & perception (high) vs internally focused or unsure (low).

Subtopic02.4.question: Can you tell me about a time you had to keep a client or external partner informed while something important was changing behind the scenes?
Subtopic02.4.purpose: Proactive communication: seeks input and shares openly (high) vs communicates only when required (low). Assess how they manage external communications and build trust during uncertainty or change.

Subtopic02.5.purpose: Transparency and alignment: keeps partners informed and aligned (high) vs withholds detail or focus is narrow (low).

Subtopic02.6.purpose: Integrity and trust-building: builds rapport and follows through (high) vs transactional or inconsistent (low).

Subtopic03.1.question: The challenges you have mentioned, what needs to change to address these issues? (It could be a process, a system, or how people work together.)
Subtopic03.1.purpose: Attitude toward change: actively looks to improve and evolve (high) vs cautious, uncertain, or resistant (low). Assess openness to change and whether transformation is seen as a chance to improve or a disruption to avoid.

Subtopic03.2.purpose: Response to challenges: change is an opportunity for progress (high) vs change is a disruption or threat (low).

Subtopic03.3.question: Have you seen a change that worked well, or one that didn't? What made the difference?
Subtopic03.3.purpose: Change leadership: a driver or advocate for change (high) vs passive participant or obstructive (low). Gauge their skill, capabilities, and desire to navigate and drive change.

Subtopic03.4.purpose: Confidence and capabilities: positive to change complexity (high) vs views change as trivial or as overwhelming (low).

Subtopic04.1.question: Can you give me an example of when you or your superior has changed a way of working so that things noticeably improved? What lesson did you learn from this experience?
Subtopic04.1.purpose: Focus on process improvement: identifies inefficiencies & improvements (high) vs accepts current systems without question (low). Surface practical awareness of what works (or doesn't) and opportunities for process improvement.

Subtopic04.2.purpose: Results-oriented thinking: prioritises outcomes & continuous refinement (high) vs follows routine without challenge (low).

Subtopic04.3.purpose: Contribution to performance culture: ownership of continuous improvement (high) vs passive or disengaged (low).

Subtopic04.4.question: Give me an example of how you have inspired teams to work better together?
Subtopic04.4.purpose: Driving efficiency leadership skills: challenges the status quo (high) vs accepts existing ways of working (low). Calibrate leadership capability and assess their drive and urgency to lead meaningful improvement.

Subtopic04.5.purpose: Inspiration & listening skills: engages others to drive meaningful change (high) vs directive, dismissive, or disengaged (low).

Subtopic05.1.question: Can you share when working with another team helped you or your team reach an important goal?
Subtopic05.1.purpose: Cross-functional collaboration: values interdepartmental cooperation (high) vs hesitant, guarded, or works in silos (low). Explore willingness and ability to work across teams and share ownership of broader outcomes.

Subtopic05.2.purpose: Accountability and delegation: shares responsibility & coordinates (high) vs avoids delegation or works alone (low).

Subtopic05.3.question: Think about the best leader you've worked with: what parts of their management style shows up in how you lead today?
Subtopic05.3.purpose: Authenticity: describes values they aspire to (high) vs describes values inconsistent with them (low). Understand what leadership and culture they value, and how they model it.

Subtopic05.4.purpose: Communication open & engage: open, clear, and responsive (high) vs guarded, vague, or resistant (low).

Subtopic05.5.purpose: Understanding of corporate culture: sees culture as key to performance (high) vs views culture as irrelevant or unimportant (low).

Subtopic06.1.question: Before we finish, is there anything you'd like us to ensure is built into the plans for the future? Is there anything important that you think we have missed?
Subtopic06.1.purpose: Point of Concern 1 / Emotional investment & concern: expresses strong / focused concerns (high) vs limited concerns or indifferent (low). Capture unstructured concerns, hopes, or messages and any red flags that might have been missed.

Subtopic06.2.purpose: Point of Concern 2: capture a second concern topic and comments; emotional investment and any red flags.

Subtopic06.3.purpose: Point of Concern 3: capture a third concern topic and comments; emotional investment and any red flags.
